title: Fix Grid Levels, Open Orders, and Trade History showing empty data

requirements.summary: Fix Grid Preview Table panel to correctly fetch and display grid price levels with proper loading, error, and empty state handling
acceptanceCriteria: Grid levels panel shows real buy/sell price levels when the bot has an active grid configured
A loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched
An error message is displayed if the fetch fails, rather than an empty list
Backend query for grid levels returns structured data including price levels, not an empty array, when grid is active

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the useGridLevels query hook to ensure: (1) the actor is fully initialized before the query fires, (2) canister health-check guards do not incorrectly block this query, (3) polling interval is active, and (4) fetch errors are properly propagated to the UI. Remove any conditions that prevent the query from running when the actor is ready. Ensure the query refetchInterval is active and the enabled condition checks both actor existence and health status correctly.

Update the component to properly handle three distinct states: (1) loading state with skeletons when isLoading is true, (2) error state with a visible error message when isError is true, and (3) empty state only when data is successfully fetched but contains zero grid levels. Ensure the component does not conflate fetch failures with empty results. Add conditional rendering to show the appropriate UI based on query state (isLoading, isError, isEmpty).

requirements.summary: Fix Open Orders panel to correctly fetch and display open orders with proper loading, error, and empty state handling
acceptanceCriteria: Open orders panel shows all currently placed buy and sell orders when orders exist on the canister
A loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched
An error message is displayed if the fetch fails, rather than an empty list
Backend query for open orders returns the correct list of active orders

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the useOpenOrders query hook to ensure: (1) the actor is fully initialized before the query fires, (2) canister health-check guards do not incorrectly block this query, (3) polling interval is active, and (4) fetch errors are properly propagated to the UI. Remove any conditions that prevent the query from running when the actor is ready. Ensure the query refetchInterval is active and the enabled condition checks both actor existence and health status correctly.

Update the component to properly handle three distinct states: (1) loading state with skeletons when isLoading is true, (2) error state with a visible error message when isError is true, and (3) empty state only when data is successfully fetched but contains zero open orders. Ensure the component does not conflate fetch failures with empty results. Add conditional rendering to show the appropriate UI based on query state (isLoading, isError, isEmpty).

requirements.summary: Fix Trade History panel to correctly fetch and display past trades with proper loading, error, and empty state handling
acceptanceCriteria: Trade history panel shows past trades (filled, cancelled, open) in reverse-chronological order when history exists
A loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched
An error message is displayed if the fetch fails, rather than an empty list
Backend query for trade history returns actual trade records, not an empty array, when trades have occurred

file_operations.description: Audit and fix the useTradeHistory query hook to ensure: (1) the actor is fully initialized before the query fires, (2) canister health-check guards do not incorrectly block this query, (3) polling interval is active, and (4) fetch errors are properly propagated to the UI. Remove any conditions that prevent the query from running when the actor is ready. Ensure the query refetchInterval is active and the enabled condition checks both actor existence and health status correctly.

Update the component to properly handle three distinct states: (1) loading state with skeletons when isLoading is true, (2) error state with a visible error message when isError is true, and (3) empty state only when data is successfully fetched but contains zero trade history records. Ensure the component does not conflate fetch failures with empty results. Add conditional rendering to show the appropriate UI based on query state (isLoading, isError, isEmpty).

requirements.summary: Audit useQueries.ts hooks for grid levels, open orders, and trade history to ensure proper actor initialization, query execution, and error propagation
acceptanceCriteria: Queries for grid levels, open orders, and trade history are not gated behind conditions that permanently prevent them from running
Errors from these queries are reflected in the isError state and surfaced to the corresponding panel components
Polling is active for all three queries while the dashboard is open

file_operations.description: Perform a comprehensive audit of the useGridLevels, useOpenOrders, and useTradeHistory query hooks. Verify that: (1) each query's enabled condition only checks for actor existence and does not incorrectly depend on canister health status in a way that blocks data queries, (2) each query has an active refetchInterval for polling, (3) query functions properly handle and throw errors rather than swallowing them, and (4) the useActor dependency is correctly tracked so queries refetch when the actor changes. If health checks are blocking these queries, adjust the enabled logic to allow queries to run and surface their own errors independently of health status.
